Title: Malaysian charged over 5kg methamphetamine seizure in Sydney Airport
Article:
SYDNEY: Australian police have charged a Malaysian over an alleged attempt to import methamphetamine that was found concealed in luggage in Sydney Airport, reported Xinhua.
The Australian Federal Police (AFP) and Australian Border Force (ABF) said in a joint statement on Friday that the 66-year-old man was sent for a baggage examination upon arrival in Sydney on a flight from Thailand on Thursday.
An x-ray of the man's suitcase identified anomalies and a subsequent search located 10 bottles, which were labelled as scented powder, containing 5kg of methamphetamine.
The Malaysian man was arrested and charged with one count of attempting to import a commercial quantity of a border-controlled drug.
If convicted, he faces a maximum penalty of life imprisonment.
The AFP and ABF said it would be alleged in court that the man told officers he had been recruited through social media to courier the suitcase from Thailand to Australia in exchange for payment.
Two mobile phones were seized by investigators for forensic examination.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTViral: 'Moo-ve over': Buffaloes stray onto highway fast lane [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An adult buffalo and its calf wandering onto a busy highway have sparked safety concerns, as questions grow over how the animals ended up on the road.
A video circulating on Threads shows the pair walking along the fast lane, with the calf closely trailing its mother.
A female voice from inside a stationary vehicle can be heard expressing alarm as traffic moves past.
View on Threads
"Hey, don't hit them!" she shouts as a motorcycle rides by, repeatedly referring to the calf as "the baby".
She also questions where the animals' owners are and suggests that the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) should be alerted.
Another female voice urges the driver to remain still, allowing the animals to pass safely.
In the background, a male voice is heard asking whether such incidents are common in Malaysia.
The unusual sight has since prompted light-hearted reactions online, with some joking that the buffaloes had joined 'balik kampung' traffic ahead of Hari Raya.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTD strengthens advocacy in road safety campaign for Aidilfitri
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Road Transport Department is intensifying the implementation of a road safety campaign in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri with a more strategic, comprehensive and high-impact approach to cultivate prudent driving among the public.
Its director-general, Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli, said the campaign this year presents a new narrative that emphasises the delivery of safety messages in a more aggressive, creative, and inclusive manner through various platforms, including mainstream and digital media.
"For the first time, RTD also produced a special Hari Raya Aidilfitri advertisement filled with safety messages, delivered in a relaxed manner, and close to the hearts of the community to enhance the effectiveness of the message," he said in a statement.
He said the campaign is also strengthened through a strategic collaboration with well-known celebrities and influencers such as Datuk Sri Siti Nurhaliza, Zizan Razak, Izzue Islam and Boss James, who helped convey the safety message to various layers of society.
At the same time, Aedy Fadly also said RTD does not overlook enforcement aspects, but instead balances it with focused operations in hotspot areas, the implementation of road bans on goods vehicles, and the use of drone technology to enhance monitoring on major highways.
"This approach emphasises that road safety cannot rely solely on enforcement actions, but rather needs to be strengthened through awareness, enhancement of understanding, and continuous attitude changes among road users, especially during festive seasons," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police arrest man after 15km Myvi chase ends in crash
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested a local man in his 20s after a 15km high-speed chase along the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) ended in a crash on Wednesday.
A video of the incident went viral on social media.
Petaling Jaya police chief, Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat, said the incident occurred at 2.10pm when a patrol car attempted to stop a blue Perodua Myvi for inspection, but the driver sped off instead.
"The suspect drove aggressively and attempted to evade police, triggering a pursuit that stretched 15 kilometres towards Klang," he said in a statement.
During the chase, the suspect rammed several other vehicles before losing control and crashing. He was subsequently apprehended by police.
"Checks revealed the suspect, a local with seven prior criminal records, is also wanted for an offence under Section 15(4) of the Prevention of Crime Act (POCA) in Kuala Bera. Investigations also found that the vehicle he was driving had been reported stolen in Kuala Bera based on its chassis number," he added.
The case is being investigated under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant and Section 279 for reckless driving.
"The suspect has been remanded for two days until March 20 and will be handed over to investigators in Bera for further action. We urge anyone who witnessed the incident to come forward and assist the investigation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 12,000 litres of coconut milk sold out in five hours
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: Approximately 12,000 litres of coconut milk were sold out in just five hours at Pasar Cabang Tiga here, forcing many shoppers to settle for whole coconuts or grated coconut instead.
A survey found customers, mostly housewives, joined long queues for the supply as six out of seven traders were forced to close early after running out of old coconuts.
Trader Wan Yusof Wan Muda, 70, said the 12,000 litres were produced from 20 tonnes of old coconuts starting early this morning, with crowds forming as early as 6am.
"We secured 20,000 old coconuts, or about 20 tonnes, from suppliers in several states, including Perak, last week. The public response for santan this Aidilfitri has been extraordinary," said Wan Yusof, who has operated at the market for 45 years.
He noted that demand this year significantly surpassed last year's festive period.
Another trader, Wan Mohd Sukri Wan Muda, 52, said he had to limit sales to two kg per customer to ensure those waiting in line could obtain a supply.
"We received 20,000 old coconuts last Sunday. We did not expect such an overwhelming response," he said, adding that all traders complied with the RM16 per kg ceiling price set by the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold set for third weekly fall on hawkish US Fed, elevated dollar
Article:
BENGALURU: Gold prices edged up on Friday on technical buying, but were headed for a third consecutive weekly decline, pressured by a firm US dollar and as a hawkish US Federal Reserve dampened hopes for near-term interest rate cuts.
Spot gold rose 0.6 per cent to US$4,675.23 per ounce as of 0702 GMT, rebounding from a near two-month low hit in the previous session.
US gold futures for April delivery rose 1.6 per cent to US$4,676.90.
"Gold held some important technical supports in the weekly time frame and gold may see a recovery to the level where it broke down, around US$4,800," said Nicholas Frappell, global head of institutional markets at ABC Refinery.
However, bullion has lost over six per cent so far this week. Spot gold has fallen more than 10 per cent since the US-Israeli strike on Iran on February 28.
The dollar has emerged as one of the clearest "safe-haven" winners, strengthening over two per cent so far this month. Meanwhile, the Fed kept rates steady on Wednesday and indicated that inflation could rise.
Interest rate futures show traders see little chance of a Fed reduction this year, according to the CME's FedWatch tool.
Gold is considered an inflation hedge, but high interest rates turn yield-bearing assets more attractive, while a stronger dollar makes the bullion more expensive for holders of other currencies.
"After notable underperformance during the Middle East conflict, participants were poised to sell rather than buy gold and were listening out for a reason to confirm their sentiments," Frappell said.
Oil prices fell on Friday but stayed above US$105 a barrel as Israel launched further attacks on Tehran, a day after President Donald Trump told it not to repeat its strikes on Iranian natural gas infrastructure, sharply escalating the war.
On Thursday, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US may soon remove sanctions from Iranian oil stranded on tankers.
Meanwhile, gold discounts in India eased this week from near decade-high levels hit last week, helped by festive buying and a sharp correction in prices, while premiums in China declined as physical demand softened.
Spot silver fell 1.7 per cent to US$71.66 per ounce. Spot platinum rose 0.2 per cent at US$1,974.45 and palladium added 1 per cent to US$1,461.3.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Smugglers who caused patrol boat to run aground linked to human trafficking syndicate
Article:
KOTA BARU: The suspected smugglers who caused a marine patrol boat to run aground on a riverbank in Tumpat yesterday were believed to be members of a human trafficking syndicate operating from Tak Bai, Thailand.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said this was based on information provided by marine policemen involved in the operation.
"We suspect the Thai smugglers were on their way to send undocumented migrants to Malaysia after picking them up from a vessel at sea.
"They were travelling along Sungai Kelantan towards the riverbank at Kampung Teluk Renjuna when the incident occurred.
"So far, we have not sought assistance from our Thai counterparts to track down the suspects as investigations are still ongoing," he added.
It was reported that a marine police boat from Kubor
ran aground
on the riverbank in Kampung Teluk Renjuna while pursuing a vessel suspected of carrying smugglers.
The incident occurred at 6am yesterday when the team spotted a fibreglass boat believed to be involved in migrant smuggling along the Kelantan River during Op Taring Landai/Op Khas Aidilfitri Phase 1/2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Crown Princess Mette-Marit reveals she was 'manipulated' by Jeffrey Epstein
Article:
OSLO: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit said she was "manipulated" by convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, in an interview broadcast Friday in which she sought to explain their close relationship.
A commoner who married Crown Prince Haakon in 2001, Mette-Marit's name appears in new Epstein documents released by the US Department of Justice earlier this year.
The files revealed an unexpectedly close friendship between the pair and raised questions in Norway about whether Mette-Marit can become queen.
"Of course I wish I had never met him," she told public broadcaster NRK during the roughly 20-minute interview.
"It is extremely important for me to acknowledge that I did not look into his past more carefully, and also to acknowledge that I was manipulated and deceived to such an extent," she said.
She also sought to squash speculation about the nature of her relationship to Epstein, who pleaded guilty in 2008 to soliciting a minor for prostitution and died in 2019 while awaiting trial for sex trafficking.
"It was a friendly relationship: above all, he was a friend to me. But if your question is whether the relationship had another nature, the answer is no," she said.
Mette-Marit has previously said that she deeply regretted her relationship with the disgraced financier.
The revelations about Epstein have damaged her reputation, at a time when she is dealing with a trial of her 29-year-old son from a relationship before she married the crown prince.
Marius Borg Hoiby faces 38 charges, including raping four women and assaulting ex-girlfriends, and faces up to 16 years in prison.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Safeguard harmony, do not let small disputes spiral into chaos
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: The beauty of Malaysia lies in its people of diverse races and religions living together in harmony, and this unity must be preserved by avoiding infighting that could lead to chaos, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said that while minor disagreements are inevitable in a multiracial society, they must be managed swiftly and wisely to prevent escalation.
"Malaysia is peaceful today, and we must ensure it remains so. Even small disputes must be handled quickly.
"If necessary, take firm action — do not compromise until the situation becomes uncontrollable," he said in his speech during a gathering with local leaders and the community here today.
Anwar warned that unchecked conflicts could have severe consequences, including economic collapse, job losses and threats to lives.
"If fights are allowed to grow, the country can be destroyed, the economy will suffer, people will lose their jobs, and even lives could be lost," he said, urging Malaysians not to take the current stability for granted.
Anwar also stressed the importance of cooperation across communities and political divides, especially during challenging economic conditions influenced by global factors.
He said law enforcement authorities, including the police, had acted promptly in mediating recent disputes involving representatives from different religious groups.
"If there are differences, resolve them according to the law. If an offence is committed, then prosecute — that is a separate matter.
"But do not let the country descend into chaos," he warned.
Anwar called on Malaysians, regardless of background, to exercise restraint and mutual respect, emphasising that dissatisfaction exists in every community but should be addressed through dialogue.
"There may be dissatisfaction among Malays, Chinese, Indians, and those in Sabah and Sarawak — that is normal.
"What is important is that we resolve it properly through discussion," he added.
Anwar also urged leaders to prioritise the welfare of the people over personal interests, while encouraging the public to practise moderation in spending amid global economic uncertainties.
Despite global pressures affecting currencies worldwide, Anwar said Malaysia's position remained relatively stable and should be safeguarded.
"Be grateful and protect what we have. Do not assume this stability will last forever," he said.
Highlighting examples of mutual understanding among communities, Anwar pointed to how different groups respect one another's religious practices, describing it as a defining strength of the nation.
He shared examples of mutual understanding among different communities, especially in respecting religious practices.
He noted how non-Muslims show consideration during the fasting month, even at events where food and drinks are not served out of respect for Muslims who are fasting.
"This is the beauty of Malaysia. Chinese and Indian friends who are not fasting do not complain about the absence of food or drinks. That understanding is something many people do not fully appreciate," he said.
He also cited how a Chinese traders' association in Kuala Lumpur adjusted the timing of its celebration to accommodate Muslim sensitivities, holding it before Chinese New Year so that Muslim attendees could comfortably participate.
"As a Malay, I truly appreciate and feel proud of such friends. They understand us, and we should respond in the same spirit. That is how tolerance grows among us," he said.
Anwar added that such mutual respect must be reciprocated.
He shared how he attended an appreciation event for Indian workers ahead of the Thaipusam celebration, ensuring respect for their customs while remaining within Islamic guidelines.
"I went earlier to show appreciation and respect, while not participating in religious rituals.
"That is how we maintain balance — respecting others while remaining true to our own beliefs," he explained.
He also expressed appreciation to Hindu community leaders for their understanding, noting that both sides must continue to respect one another's boundaries.
"If we continue in this spirit of mutual respect, the country will remain safe," he said.
Reiterating his message, Anwar stressed that unity must be preserved, especially amid economic pressures and security challenges.
"This is crucial to ensure our country remains peaceful, stable and resilient.
"This is the beauty of Malaysia — people understand and respect each other's traditions.
"That spirit of tolerance is what keeps the country safe and harmonious," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three dead, five injured in road crash in Sri Aman
Article:
KUCHING: Three people were killed and five others injured in an accident involving two vehicles near Kampung Selepong Jinggau, Sri Aman, about 8.30am today.
The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department in a statement said the accident involved a Proton X70 and a Perodua Alza, the latter of which was completely destroyed by fire.
"The three victims in the Proton X70 comprising a 70-year-old man and two women, both aged 22, were confirmed dead at the scene by the medical team.
"Two other passengers in the same vehicle, a 6-year-old boy and a 4-year-old boy, suffered head injuries and were sent to the Sri Aman Hospital for further treatment while the bodies of the three deceased victims have been handed over to the police for further action," said the department.
Meanwhile, three victims in the Perodua Alza, comprising an adult woman and two boys, managed to escape with minor injuries before the vehicle burst into flames and the victims were taken to hospital for treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms expected in four states until 5pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds in Penang, Perak, Pahang, and Sarawak until 5pm today.
MetMalaysia said in a statement that the affected areas in Penang are North Seberang Perai, Central Seberang Perai, and South Seberang Perai, while in Pahang, the warning covered Cameron Highlands and Lipis.
The areas in Perak include Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, and Batang Padang.
Meanwhile, in Sarawak, the areas under the alert are Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Daro, Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), and Kapit (Song and Kapit).
Authorities advised the public to remain vigilant and avoid outdoor activities during the period of heavy rain and strong winds.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR slams Hamzah's statement, says targeted diesel subsidy needs to go on
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's statement on the diesel subsidy issue does not reflect the overall current economic facts and has the potential to mislead the people, especially when the government is implementing the restructuring of fuel subsidies.
PKR deputy secretary-general Sivamalar Genapathy said the statement repeats an old narrative that was emotional without taking into account the need for subsidy reform to ensure the country's fiscal sustainability.
She said the previous subsidy system was exposed to serious leakages that cost billions of ringgit every year.
"When they (the opposition) were given the mandate to govern, this leakage issue was left to linger without thorough correction. The targeted implementation of diesel subsidies is important to close the leakage and ensure that the subsidy is only enjoyed by those who truly deserve it.
"The claim that the diesel price adjustment will burden the people must be viewed responsibly," she said in a statement today.
Sivamalar said the targeted implementation of diesel subsidies also ensures that critical sectors such as logistics, public transport and agriculture continue to be protected through a special mechanism introduced by the government.
She said the government still maintained the subsidised price of RON95 at RM1.99 per litre compared to the market price of RON97 at around RM3.85 as a measure to protect the majority of the people, despite facing pressure on global oil prices following the conflict in the Middle East.
She added that the government had also implemented various mitigation measures to control the chain effect on the cost of living, including the initial payment of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) amounting to RM1.3 billion to 5.2 million recipients ahead of Aidilfitri.
In addition, special assistance was also channeled to civil servants, retirees and veterans, as well as appreciation to more than 70,000 imams, bilals, Kafa teachers and mosque management staff.
Sivamalar said the proposal to postpone the implementation of subsidy reforms may sound popular, but it does not resolve the fundamental issues and only reflects an approach without a clear direction.
"If you really want to protect the people, present a clear, comprehensive and implementable mitigation plan, not just cause concern or create narrow perceptions," she said.
Hamzah had previously said the increase in diesel prices by 80 sen to RM4.72 per litre
should not be implemented
at this time, but postponed until after Aidilfitri or done in stages.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Govt closely monitors security, economic pressures
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government had stepped up monitoring of both national security and economic risks as global tensions intensified, warning that overseas conflicts were increasingly affecting Malaysia directly.
Speaking at a get-together with the local leader and people of Permatang Pauh here today, Anwar said the police, the armed forces and relevant agencies had been placed on heightened alert following the recent National Security Council meeting.
"I want to stress that this is important — to protect our country at a time when economic pressures and national security challenges are very heavy," he said.
Anwar cautioned against complacency, noting that geopolitical instability — particularly in the Middle East — was no longer remote.
"Do not think that all these conflicts are far from us. The anger elsewhere has effects here. That is why we must continue to monitor closely," he added.
He said Malaysia had been in contact with several world leaders, including counterparts from Iran, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Turkiye and India, as tensions escalated following exchanges involving Israel and Iran.
Anwar added that the situation had worsened with missile and drone strikes affecting military installations across multiple countries and spilling into civilian areas.
"Tensions have now spread across the region. Even the Prime Minister of India contacted me, asking if Malaysia could help explore ways to reduce the situation," he said, attributing the outreach to Malaysia's independent diplomatic stance.
On the economic front, Anwar said disruptions to global energy supply chains — including reported closures of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on oil and gas infrastructure — had driven a sharp rise in global oil prices.
"Oil prices have soared. Previously they were controlled, but now with the closure of key routes and attacks on oil and gas facilities, prices have risen significantly," he said.
Despite this, Malaysia had maintained the RON95 petrol price at RM1.99 per litre, among the lowest globally, due to continued government intervention.
"Malaysia is still holding at RM1.99. Every day we monitor the situation closely," he said.
Anwar added that Putrajaya had convened frequent high-level meetings involving the police, military leadership and key ministers, alongside special cabinet sessions, to coordinate responses to both security and economic developments.
He warned that the broader economic impact was already evident, noting that some countries, including Thailand, had begun rationing fuel.
He added that Cambodia had sought fuel assistance from Malaysia and Singapore, while fuel prices in Singapore had surged sharply.
Although diesel and RON97 prices in Malaysia had also increased, Anwar said the country's economy remained comparatively resilient, even as other nations faced currency volatility.
"Some accept this positively, others are envious, and there are those who may want to undermine us. So we must remain cautious," he said.
Domestically, he acknowledged concerns over rising costs driven by higher fertiliser prices, warning of potential knock-on effects on food supply and inflation.
"When fertiliser prices rise, farmers are affected and this leads to higher prices of goods. That is our concern," he noted, stressing that existing stockpiles were being used to cushion short-term pressures.
He said discussions with Petronas indicated current mitigation measures could sustain stability for the next few months, possibly until May, but cautioned that prolonged conflict could strain supplies further.
"We can manage for now because of proper planning, but this can only be sustained for a few months," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sri Lanka refused ground access to US warplanes
Article:
COLOMBO: Sri Lanka refused permission to the United States to station two of its warplanes at an airport in the island's south in early March, President Anura Kumara Dissanayake told parliament on Friday.
"They wanted to bring two warplanes armed with eight anti-ship missiles from a base in Djibouti to the Mattala International Airport from March 4 to 8 and we said 'no'," Dissanayake said.
Sri Lanka maintains close ties with the United States, its biggest export market, and Iran which is a key buyer of tea, its main export commodity.-AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTS Link to boost Johor Baru-Singapore connectivity
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link project connecting Johor Baru and Singapore is being developed as part of a broader integrated mobility network linking the cross-border rail service with existing and future public transport systems in the city.
Mass Rapid Transit Corp Sdn Bhd (MRT Corp) chief executive officer Mohd Zarif Hashim said integration had been a key guiding principle in the planning of the RTS Link from the outset.
"The RTS Link was never conceived as a standalone service. It was designed to function as part of a wider mobility network for Johor Baru and the surrounding region," he told Bernama.
He said under the plan, the future Autonomous Rail Transit (ART) system in Johor Baru would include a stop at Bukit Chagar, with a direct connection to the RTS Link, and a site for the ART station had already been identified.
To realise this integrated transport vision, Mohd Zarif said MRT Corp had been working closely with the Transport Ministry, the Johor state government, Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ), the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), as well as the management of Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) to develop Bukit Chagar into a fully integrated transport hub.
Mohd Zarif said that, as part of efforts to strengthen first- and last-mile connectivity, more than 250 additional buses were expected to be introduced by 2027 to enhance public bus services in the city.
Another key component is a pedestrian overhead bridge that would provide direct connections between the RTS station and rail services such as the KTM Komuter and the Electric Train Service (ETS), helping to distribute passenger flow beyond the Bukit Chagar area.
He said accessibility is central to MRT Corp's customer experience vision, ensuring commuters could reach, enter and use the system seamlessly from the start of their journey.
"When transfers and access points are intuitive and well connected, public transport becomes part of everyday life rather than a mode that requires extra consideration," he said.
Mohd Zarif said that when the RTS Link begins operations after construction is completed on Dec 31, the transit-oriented development (TOD) at Bukit Chagar was expected to still be under construction.
He said that during this period, commuters might experience traffic flow adjustments, making clear and timely information crucial to ensure a smooth and reliable travel experience.
The pedestrian connectivity would link the station to surrounding developments, including the Sultan Ibrahim Building, while park-and-ride facilities would provide additional options for commuters, he added.
These included a 1,500-bay park-and-ride facility at Bukit Chagar offering both standard and premium parking, as well as 700 RTS-designated parking bays at Coronation Square, he said.
Mohd Zarif said direct pedestrian bridges, including connections to the Johor Baru Sentral (JB Sentral) KTM station, would further enhance accessibility and extend connectivity across Greater Johor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US agency deepens probe into Tesla self-driving technology
Article:
WASHINGTON: The United States (US) road safety authority said it was deepening its investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system over concerns about its performance in poor visibility, reported German Press Agency (dpa).
Analysis of crash data suggests the technology may have problems under such conditions, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said on Thursday.
Tesla chief Elon Musk has pushed for vehicles to operate autonomously using only cameras as sensors.
Competitors and many experts have warned that cameras alone may not be sufficiently reliable, particularly in difficult visibility conditions. However, Musk continues to stand by his approach.
Other developers of self-driving vehicles, including robotaxi leader Waymo, a subsidiary of Google parent Alphabet, also use more expensive technologies such as laser radar systems to scan a vehicle's surroundings.
If Musk's approach proves successful, Tesla could gain a significant cost advantage.
The NHTSA is examining two versions of the system, known as Full Self-Driving (FSD). Despite the name, it currently functions only as a driver assistance system, with drivers required to remain in control at all times.
Tesla refers to one version as "supervised" FSD, which must be monitored by users. A more advanced version, in which vehicles are intended to operate autonomously, is so far available in the US only as a beta test.
According to the agency, Tesla has said the FSD system is designed to detect when cameras encounter visibility issues and to alert drivers.
However, in the crashes under review, the system did not recognise when cameras were impaired – for example by glare or airborne particles – and did not issue warnings.
As a result, drivers did not have sufficient time to react, the NHTSA said.
The investigation covers Tesla models produced from 2016 onwards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar joins congregants for Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today joined the local community for Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin in Kampung Pelet.
He arrived at 1.15pm and was received by Permatang Pauh Umno division chief Datuk Mohd Zaidi Mohd Said and Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid.
Anwar and the congregation of approximately 700 people listened to a sermon titled 'Ramadan, ketika senja terakhir berlabuh' (Ramadan, as the final twilight sets), delivered by the mosque's imam, Muhammad Nasir Hamid.
The sermon reminded congregants of the importance of Ramadan practices, describing the holy month as the best opportunity to seek the grace and forgiveness of Allah.
It also urged the public to emulate the character of the salafus soleh (pious predecessors) by filling the month with devotion and maintaining consistency in worship beyond seasonal observance.
After the prayers, congregants took the opportunity to greet and take selfies with the prime minister.
Anwar also spent time mingling with the crowd, enquiring about their preparations for the upcoming Aidilfitri celebrations.
Earlier in the morning, the prime minister conducted a community visit, touring several houses in the vicinity on foot.
He is expected to conclude his working visit by attending a community leaders' session at the Permatang Pasir public hall.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's unsubsidised fuel price increase reflects global trends – Analyst
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Recent increases in unsubsidised fuel prices in Malaysia reflect rising global oil prices, according to an analyst.
Geostrategist and Senior Fellow at the Nusantara Academy of Strategic Research (NASR), Prof Dr Azmi Hassan said the increase in global oil prices from US$60-US$65 per barrel previously to around US$110 currently has made price adjustments unavoidable.
He said while the public generally understands that fuel prices have risen due to higher global oil prices, there remains a perception that prices should not rise as Malaysia is an oil-producing country.
"While Malaysia exports crude oil and earns revenue, it still imports refined fuel for domestic use, making it exposed to global price movements," he told Bernama.
He said targeted subsidy measures such as the subsidised RON95 petrol price under the BUDI MADANI RON95 (BUDI95) initiative help mitigate the impact of rising fuel costs.
"Initiatives like BUDI95 can control the cost of logistics for ordinary people. The RM1.99 price is basically a giveaway to the people," he said.
He was referring to the subsidised RON95 petrol price under the BUDI95 initiative, which remains at RM1.99 per litre despite global crude oil prices exceeding US$100 per barrel following the conflict in West Asia.
At the same time, Azmi cautioned that the current subsidy bill, estimated at RM3.2 billion monthly, may not be sustainable in the long term.
"We just can't enjoy the RM1.99 forever, with increasing oil prices worldwide," he said.
He added that Malaysia remains in a relatively stronger position compared to some countries, noting that inefficiencies in other oil-producing nations have affected their fuel supply systems.
Meanwhile, Azmi said differences in fuel pricing between regions are based on usage patterns, noting that diesel remains subsidised in Sabah and Sarawak due to its widespread use.
"The government is still maintaining lower diesel prices in Sabah and Sarawak due to the fact that RON95 in the peninsula is maintained at RM1.99. Diesel is widely use in Borneo states not only for logistical purposes, but also by both the private and public sectors.
"That's why diesel has been subsidised in Sabah and Sarawak. On the other hand, the widely used fuel in the peninsula is petrol," he said.
Azmi stressed that any increase in diesel prices in Sabah and Sarawak could have a more severe impact on living costs.
"If there is an increase from RM2.15 in Sabah, even by 10 or 20 sen, there will be an impact on the cost of living because the wide usage of diesel in all segments of life in Borneo.
"The impact is going to be severe in Sabah and Sarawak in terms of higher cost of living and expenses," he said.
Amid rising global oil prices triggered by US-Iran conflict, the Ministry of Finance (MoF) announced on March 12 an increase in unsubsidised fuel prices in Peninsular Malaysia.
From March 12 to March 18, retail prices for unsubsidised petrol and diesel were adjusted, with RON97 rising by 60 sen to RM3.85 per litre and unsubsidised RON95 increasing by 60 sen to RM3.27 per litre, while diesel rose by 80 sen to RM3.92 per litre.
In the latest revision effective March 19 to March 25, RON97 increased further to RM4.55 per litre, while diesel in Peninsular Malaysia rose to RM4.72 per litre. Unsubsidised RON95 remained unchanged at RM3.27 per litre.
Meanwhile, diesel prices in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan remain unchanged at RM2.15 per litre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah Electricity ensures minimal power disruptions during Aidilfitri
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah utility authorities have ensured minimal power disruptions during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebration.
Sabah Electricity chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Yaakob Jaafar said among the measures taken is the deployment of 500 personnel to remain on duty throughout the festive period.
The workforce, comprising engineers, technicians and operations staff, was deployed across key facilities, including power stations, substations and system control centres statewide.
"The commitment shown by our personnel, even on a festive morning, reflected the company's priority in safeguarding public well-being.
"While Aidilfitri is a time for family, their dedication ensured the continuity of stable electricity supply, especially during periods of increased demand," he said in a statement.
He added that operational preparedness during the festive season had been enhanced through close coordination among technical teams on the ground and centralised system monitoring to ensure swift and effective handling of any incidents.
Mohd Yaakob also advised the public to prioritise electrical safety and practise efficient energy use, particularly during the festive period.
Consumers could obtain safety tips and energy-saving guidelines via the company's official website and customer service channels, including its CareLine, hotline and mobile application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Diesel drums, tyre pile destroyed in Sabah fire
Article:
KIMANIS: A fire involving diesel drums and a tyre pile at Batu 30, Kimanis, was brought under control after an overnight operation by the Fire and Rescue Department.
The blaze, reported at 6.20pm yesterday, saw firefighters from Kimanis and Papar stations arrive within 20 minutes.
The fire was contained by 11pm and operations ended at 8.20am.
According to the operations commander, the fire involved six diesel drums, three of which contained fuel.
The drums had a maximum capacity of 18,000 litres each.
Of the affected units, one drum contained 18,000 litres of diesel, another 10,000 litres, and a third about 1,000 litres.
Firefighters managed to save two of the fuel-filled drums, including the one containing the highest volume.
However, the third drum was about 70 per cent destroyed in the blaze.
A tyre pile of the size of two full basketball courts was completely destroyed.
Teams also prevented the fire from spreading to a nearby workers' quarters and office.
A total of 16 personnel battled the blaze using water from a nearby river, with assistance from heavy machinery.
No injuries were reported, and the cause of the fire is under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Happiness is built through care, solidarity and purpose
Article:
Today, as the world marks the International Day of Happiness, we are reminded that happiness is not merely an individual pursuit but a shared global aspiration that must be nurtured through compassion, justice, and collective responsibility.
This year's observance comes at a time when the world is confronted with multiple and overlapping challenges — ongoing conflicts, economic uncertainty, rising cost of living, climate change, and increasing mental health concerns.
These realities weigh heavily on individuals, families, and communities, affecting not only livelihoods but also emotional and psychological well-being.
In such a climate, the meaning of happiness must be redefined. It is no longer about fleeting joy or material success, but about resilience, solidarity, and a sense of purpose.
True happiness lies in knowing that we are not alone — that we care for one another, support the vulnerable, and build communities grounded in empathy and trust.
To achieve happiness in today's context, several key principles must guide us:
First, we must prioritise mental health and emotional well-being. Seeking help should never be seen as a weakness, and societies must continue to break the stigma surrounding mental health issues.
Second, we must strengthen social cohesion. Acts of kindness, volunteerism, and community engagement can go a long way in restoring hope and connection among people.
Third, we must promote fairness and inclusivity. Inequality and injustice are major barriers to happiness. Ensuring that no one is left behind is essential in building a content and harmonious society.
Fourth, we must adopt sustainable living. Protecting our environment is not only about safeguarding the planet, but also about securing the well-being and happiness of future generations.
Finally, we must cultivate gratitude and mindfulness in our daily lives. Even in difficult times, finding meaning in small acts and simple moments can help us stay grounded and hopeful.
As we reflect on the true essence of happiness, let us remember that it is built not in isolation, but through our relationships, our values, and our shared humanity.
Let us strive to create a society where happiness is not a privilege for a few, but a right for all.
"Happiness is not found in what we have, but in how we care, how we share, and how we uplift one another in times of need."
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
Alliance for a Safe Community Chairman
Kuala Lumpur
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Gulf states come under drone, missile attacks
Article:
KUWAIT CITY: Drone attacks caused fire at Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery on Friday, state media reported, as authorities in several Gulf states said they were responding to attacks from Iran.
Iran has stepped up attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure in recent days, hitting refineries and the world's biggest gas hub in Qatar, as Teheran retaliated over Israeli strikes on its South Pars gas field.
The official Kuwait News Agency, citing the national oil company, said "several hostile drone attacks" hit the Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery, causing fires but no casualties.
It said firefighters were working to contain the blazes while "several refinery units were shut down."
Earlier on Friday, Bahrain's interior ministry said that shrapnel from an "Iranian aggression" caused a fire at a warehouse, which was brought under control and resulted in no injuries.
In Kuwait, an army statement said air defences were "responding to hostile missile and drone threats", while the UAE state media reported "incoming missile and drone threats from Iran."
Iran's Revolutionary Guards said in a statement carried by the Tasnim news agency that they had targeted US forces in the UAE's Al-Dhafra air base as well as sites in Israel with missiles and drones.
Saudi Arabia's defence ministry said that within about two hours, its forces had "intercepted and destroyed" more than a dozen drones in the country's east and another in the north.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't panic buy, Mat Sabu tells Malaysians amid global strains [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians have been urged not to panic buy, with the government assuring that food supplies remain sufficient through the festive season until May.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said his ministry, together with relevant agencies, is monitoring stock levels, prices and supply chains on a daily basis.
He said swift action would be taken if any disruptions were detected.
"The government is ready to take whatever intervention measures are necessary.
"Our priority is simple: food supplies must always be available in the market and accessible to the people," he said in his Hari Raya Aidilfitri message.
Mohamad also cautioned against overbuying based on rumours.
"Buy only according to your needs, spend wisely and avoid waste. Also, support local products," he said in his Hari Raya Aidilfitri address.
He also said that the impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is increasingly being felt globally, including rising oil prices and higher food commodity costs.
With no immediate resolution in sight, he warned that pressure on global food prices could persist.
"I want to be honest that prices and supply situations can change at any time.
"We need to prepare ourselves because today's world is full of uncertainties. A state of readiness is not a sign of fear, but a mark of maturity and foresight," he said, adding that the government would continue efforts to safeguard the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah eyes China tourism boost with new Chongqing route
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is looking to boost international arrivals with the launch of a new direct flight from Chongqing, China.
The inaugural Chongqing Airlines flight, carrying 154 passengers, landed at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) at 11.15pm yesterday.
With the addition of Chongqing, Sabah is now directly connected to eight key cities in China, including Hong Kong, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, Shanghai, Beijing, Hangzhou and Fuzhou.
According to the Sabah Tourism Board, Chinese visitors made up a significant share of international arrivals last year, accounting for 657,528 of the 1,498,709 recorded.
China Southern Airlines Kuala Lumpur office general manager Guo Hao said the new Chongqing–Kota Kinabalu route would strengthen connectivity between the two cities.
"It shortens the distance between Chongqing and Kota Kinabalu, while providing a new channel for residents to travel to Southeast Asia for tourism, business and cultural exchanges," he said in a statement.
He added that Kota Kinabalu's island attractions and tropical landscape have made it a popular destination among Chinese tourists.
Chongqing Airlines, a subsidiary of China Southern Airlines, will operate the route three times a week, on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays.
The arrival was marked with a water cannon salute coordinated by Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB), while the Sabah Tourism Board hosted a cultural welcome, with greeters in traditional attire presenting souvenirs to passengers.
Among those present were Sabah Tourism Board chief executive officer Julinus Jeffery Jimit and Tourism Malaysia Sabah director Haryanty Abu Bakar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested for recruiting schoolchildren into gangs in Seremban
Article:
SEREMBAN: Police have arrested a man suspected of recruiting school students to join gangs in the district.
The 20-year-old was detained by the state police D7 Division (Secret Societies, Gambling and Vice) together with the Seremban police D7 team in front of a restaurant in Garden Homes here at 9.10pm on Tuesday.
Seremban police chief Assistant Commissioner Azahar Abdul Rahim said the suspect has a prior criminal record involving four offences.
He said the man has been remanded for three days starting Wednesday to assist investigations under Section 45 of the Societies Act.
"Investigations are still at an early stage and police are in the process of identifying other individuals involved to have their statements recorded," he said today.
Azahar said police had earlier received a report on a dispute involving Year Six students in Seremban on March 12.
They found elements of gangsterism involving irresponsible external parties attempting to influence the students to join illegal groups.
"Police have opened an investigation paper under Section 45 of the Societies Act for further investigation and as a preventive measure to ensure the issue does not spread within the community, especially among schoolchildren," he said.
Those with information can come forward or contact the investigating officer, Inspector Ikhmal, at 06-6033222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Raya spike: Homes warned on power, water use
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The public has been urged to use electricity and water prudently during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period, when consumption of both resources typically rises.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said residential electricity demand is expected to increase by between 20 and 40 per cent compared with normal usage.
He said simple measures could help reduce consumption, including switching off lights and appliances when not in use, avoiding standby mode, setting air-conditioners between 24°C and 26°C, and choosing appliances with four- or five-star energy efficiency ratings.
Water usage is also projected to rise by 30 to 35 per cent, particularly in states that see an influx of people returning home for the festive season.
The energy transition and water transformation minister said water supply operators are on alert to ensure demand is met, while the National Water Services Commission (Spad) has prepared contingency plans for any unexpected disruptions.
The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through Span, is targeting a maximum daily per capita water usage of 160 litres, in line with international benchmarks.
Among the recommended measures are fixing leaks, using rainwater for non-essential activities such as cleaning, washing clothes only with full loads, and ensuring taps are properly closed after use.
He added that reusing uncontaminated water, such as from ablution (wuduk), and choosing products with two- or three-star water-efficiency labels could help households cut bills by up to 50%.
On electrical safety, Petra advised the public to ensure home wiring is in good condition and to test Residual Current Devices (RCDs) monthly using the 'T' button.
"If the RCD functions properly, it will trip and cut the electricity supply immediately. If it does not function, please engage a registered electrical contractor," he said.
Other precautions include avoiding overloading sockets, ensuring wires are not damaged, keeping hands dry when using electrical appliances, and not using mobile phones while charging.
Fadillah said efficient use of electricity and water would not only ensure a safer festive period but also support efforts to maintain sustainable and resilient national resources.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Diesel supply in Johor restored, monitoring continues
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Diesel supply across Johor has largely stabilised, with most petrol stations now back to normal operations following recent disruptions.
Johor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said supply recovery had progressed steadily after delayed shipments arrived and distribution to stations was expedited.
She said diesel deliveries were now being carried out in stages to ensure all affected stations receive adequate supply.
"Supply is recovering and distribution is ongoing. We expect the situation to return fully to normal levels in stages," she said.
Lilis said enforcement teams had been deployed on the ground, particularly along key routes such as Jalan Tebrau, Ulu Tiram and surrounding Johor Baru areas, where supply has already returned to normal at most stations.
"The latest updates show that nearly all petrol stations in the affected areas have resumed normal diesel supply," she said.
She added that the ministry has not received any official complaints from the public, despite earlier reports of temporary stock shortages at several stations.
At the same time, she urged consumers and transport operators to avoid panic buying, stressing that supply levels are sufficient and continuing to improve.
"We want to ensure there is no panic buying or opportunistic behaviour taking advantage of the situation," she said.
Lilis said monitoring has since been expanded statewide, covering not only stations operated by a major petrol station company but also other fuel retailers to ensure consistent supply across the board.
"Our teams are checking all stations, not just one company, to make sure supply remains stable, especially during the festive period," she said.
She added that enforcement teams remain on standby to respond to any complaints or irregularities, with the public encouraged to report issues directly to the ministry.
The recent disruption was attributed to delays in fuel shipments, which briefly affected supply at several stations before distribution resumed.
It was reported that a
diesel supply disruption
has affected several petrol stations across Johor, following delays in a fuel shipment at the Pasir Gudang Terminal.
The disruption, believed to have begun the day before yesterday, was linked to delays in a vessel carrying diesel, which affected the transfer schedule at the terminal.
Diesel stock at the terminal was expected to recover by this afternoon, while supplies at affected petrol stations are likely to be restored in stages by tomorrow.
Johor Truck Operators Association president Chai Pei Yoon said checks with the petroleum company involved found that multiple cargo ships and tankers had arrived at the port at the same time after earlier delays.
"This caused the company's tanker to queue for a long time before it could berth and unload its cargo," she said when contacted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five foreigners arrested, four in Singapore, over Southkey murder
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police have arrested five foreign men to assist in the investigation into the murder of a man in Southkey on March 14.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the 32-year-old foreign victim was attacked with a sharp weapon about 5am and was later confirmed dead at Sultan Ismail Hospital due to a stab wound to the neck.
He said several suspects believed to be involved were detected attempting to flee to Singapore after the crime.
According to him, the first suspect was apprehended by the Johor Baru South district police headquarters' D9 Division while attempting to clear immigration checks to Singapore at 12.15am on March 18.
"Four more suspects were arrested at about 6pm on March 19 with the assistance of the Singapore police," he said in a statement.
Ab Rahaman said urine tests for all suspects returned negative for drugs, and none of the men had prior criminal records.
The first suspect has been remanded for seven days starting March 18, while the remaining four have been remanded for the same duration beginning March 20.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorm, heavy rain alert for Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm today.
In a statement issued at noon, MetMalaysia said the area expected to be affected in Selangor is Sabak Bernam, while in Perak, the areas are Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak.
In Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu (Sibu) and Mukah (Daro, Matu and Dalat).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Labour market reforms key to address stagnant M40 wages
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Stagnant wage growth among the middle-income (M40) group remains a serious concern for authorities, as this demographic continues to grapple with the rising cost of living despite national economic expansion.
Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said although the economy is growing, the benefits may not yet be fully felt by the M40 group.
He said a comprehensive solution to the issue lies in labour market reforms, with the government focusing on creating high-value jobs that offer better pay.
"As the economy shifts towards higher value-added activities, it demands new skills.
"These types of jobs will automatically offer more lucrative income packages compared to low-skilled occupations," he told Berita Harian in an interview.
Abdul Rasheed said creating more high-quality jobs would encourage the entire economy to move in tandem, adding that wage increases in one sector would create positive pressure for others to follow suit.
[[nid:1401037]]
For people in the M40 who have not seen their salaries rise, he recommended reskilling and upskilling to take on value-added roles that command higher wages.
The governor also highlighted the spillover effects of the minimum wage implementation.
He said that when wages at the lowest rung are raised, salary structures at higher levels will gradually move upwards.
While the impact is not immediate, Abdul Rasheed said that these structural wage changes may take between two and three years to be fully realised.
However, he said that wage hikes must be accompanied by increased productivity to avoid inflation, which would ultimately disadvantage the people.
"Labour market reform is a long-term agenda. However, it is among the critical steps to ensure the M40 group can enjoy a better standard of living in the future," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ‘This is not the time for division,’ Anwar says as Malaysia grapples with oil price shock
Article:
SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Malaysia’s economy has remained stable despite a sharp global surge in oil prices, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, assuring the public that the government is taking measures to cushion the impact on consumers.
He explained that escalating conflict in the Middle East, attacks on oil facilities, and disruptions to key supply routes have created a volatile global market.
“Previously, prices were manageable. Now, with oil and gas installations being attacked and supply routes disrupted, prices have surged sharply,” Anwar said during a community gathering in Permatang Pauh.
The prime minister stressed that Malaysia has managed to shield consumers by maintaining the subsidised RON95 petrol price, even as prices climb steeply in neighbouring countries.
“In Singapore, petrol prices have exceeded RM10 per litre. We are still at RM1.99. This is not something that happens easily; it requires careful management,” he said, noting that Thailand has started rationing petrol while Cambodia has sought Malaysia’s help to secure its supply.
Anwar revealed that the government has been holding high-level meetings daily, including a special Cabinet session and a National Security Council meeting, and has briefed the Yang di-Pertuan Agong on the “serious economic situation.”
He added that Putrajaya is working closely with Petronas to manage reserves, which can sustain the country until at least May, while also increasing domestic production to strengthen the national buffer.
Broader economic pressures
While the government is holding the BUDI95 price, Anwar cautioned that external cost pressures are beginning to affect other areas, particularly agriculture, due to a dependency on imported fertiliser.
“If fertiliser prices rise, farmers will be affected, and that will eventually push up food prices. That is what we are trying to control now,” he said.
He also warned against opportunistic behaviour, such as exploiting supply shortages, and urged Malaysians to adopt prudent spending habits.
“Spend moderately. Save where you can. Do not assume that this situation will remain indefinitely,” he advised.
The PM stressed that domestic peace and harmony are essential for maintaining economic resilience against external shocks.
“This is not the time for division or conflict. Stability is what allows us to manage this crisis effectively,” he stated.
He also said that Malaysia is actively engaging with international partners, including Iran, Saudi Arabia, and Turkiye, to support de-escalation efforts, as continued global tensions will worsen the economic impact.
Anwar also asked the public for patience and understanding, saying that the government is doing everything possible to navigate the fragile global landscape.
“This is a difficult period. But if we remain patient, disciplined, and united, we will be able to endure,” he said.

Title: Anwar: Malaysia increasing border surveillance amid Middle East turmoil
Article:
SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today that Malaysia will heighten its border surveillance in response to growing turmoil and conflict in the Middle East.
He said the decision was made during a recent National Security Council meeting attended by the Inspector-General of Police, the Armed Forces chief, and other relevant ministers.
“All our security machinery has been instructed to remain vigilant and monitor the situation closely, especially at our borders,” Anwar said during a community gathering in Permatang Pauh here.
The prime minister said that maintaining internal stability is crucial to weathering external threats, warning that even minor domestic disputes must be handled decisively to prevent escalation.
“Even small quarrels must be addressed immediately. I have asked the police and relevant authorities to act fast. If arrests need to be made, then proceed. There must be no compromise until the situation is under control,” he asserted.
Anwar pointed to recent tensions involving different religious groups as an example where early police intervention helped facilitate dialogue and defuse a potentially chaotic situation.
“If there are offences, that is a separate matter for the courts. But we must not allow the country to descend into chaos,” he added.
Anwar noted that Malaysia’s relative stability in a time of global unrest has drawn increasing external attention.
“Other countries are facing economic turmoil, currencies are weakening, but we are still holding on,” he said.
“Because of that, there are those watching us closely; some with goodwill, some out of envy, and some who may attempt to destabilise us.”
He urged Malaysians not to be drawn into provocations and to resolve any disagreements within the framework of the law.
Reiterating the importance of unity, Anwar described Malaysia’s social harmony as both rare and valuable.
“Which other country with such diversity can remain as peaceful as this? There will always be dissatisfaction — among Malays, Chinese, Indians, Sabahans, Sarawakians — but these must be resolved through dialogue, not conflict,” he said.
He highlighted the mutual respect between communities, citing how non-Muslim groups adjust event timings during Ramadan as an example of “the beauty of Malaysia” that must be preserved.
Anwar said the government will continue its daily monitoring and coordination efforts, expressing hope for a peaceful resolution to global conflicts while stressing the need for continued vigilance at home.

Title: PM observes last day of Ramadan with Penang community ahead of Aidilfitri tomorrow
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim began his one-day working visit to Penang today by performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin in Kampung Pelet here.
He arrived at the mosque at 1.12 pm and was received by Permatang Pauh UMNO division chief Datuk Mohd Zaidi Mohd Said, mosque chairman Othman Syafie and congregants.
Anwar, together with nearly 700 worshippers, including Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid, then listened to a sermon titled “Ramadan, Ketika Senja Terakhir Berlabuh” delivered by Imam I Muhammad Nasir Hamid.
After the prayers, the Prime Minister visited the Permatang Pasir community to observe Aidilfitri preparations ahead of the nationwide celebration tomorrow.
Before concluding his visit to the Pearl of the Orient, Anwar is also scheduled to attend the “Santai Akhir Ramadan Bersama Pimpinan Komuniti Permatang Pauh” programme at the Permatang Pasir Community Hall. — Bernama

Title: JB Southkey murder: Five suspects arrested, four nabbed in Singapore with SPF’s help
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 20 — Five foreign men have been arrested for their alleged involvement in the murder of a man in the Southkey commercial area here last week.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that following the murder, investigators from the state’s Serious Crimes Unit (D9) moved quickly to identify the suspects.
Investigators arrested one of the suspects at 12.15am yesterday as he was attempting to pass through the immigration checkpoint to enter Singapore, he said in a statement today.
“Following that arrest, the remaining four suspects were apprehended in Singapore at 6pm the same day with the assistance of the Singapore Police Force (SPF).”
The arrests are linked to the killing of a 32-year-old man, who was fatally attacked with a sharp weapon at about 5am last Saturday.
The victim was pronounced dead at the Sultan Ismail Hospital (HSI) due to a stab wound to his neck.
Ab Rahaman said the first suspect apprehended in Johor has been remanded until March 24, while the four arrested in Singapore are remanded until March 26 to assist with the investigation.
He confirmed that drug screenings for all five suspects returned negative and none had prior criminal records.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which provides for the death penalty or imprisonment of 30 to 40 years and caning upon conviction.

Title: M40 yet to feel full benefits of economic growth, says BNM governor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Malaysia’s middle-income (M40) group has yet to fully benefit from economic growth, with many still facing cost of living pressures, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said.
According to a report by
Berita Harian
, he said stagnant wages among the group require labour market reforms to create higher-value jobs with better pay.
“The government’s main focus is to create high-value jobs that can offer better wages,” he was quoted as saying.
“When the economy shifts towards higher value-added activities, it requires new skills. These types of jobs will automatically offer more attractive income packages compared to low-skilled work.
“When we are able to create more high-quality jobs, this will encourage the entire economy to move together,” he reportedly added.
Abdul Rasheed reportedly encouraged the M40 group to reskill and upskill to take on higher value-added roles with better pay.
He added that increases in the minimum wage would gradually lift salary structures across the board over two to three years, placing pressure on other sectors to raise wages.
He stressed that wage growth must be supported by higher productivity, warning that increases without productivity gains could lead to inflation.
“Labour market reform is a long-term agenda. However, it is one of the critical steps to ensure the M40 group can enjoy a better standard of living in the future,” he said.

Title: Guan Eng mocks Penang CM over land tax, says 90pc revisions show system is broken
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng has demanded accountability from the state government after land tax bills were revised downward by as much as 90 per cent following errors by the Land and Mines Office.
In a statement today, the Bagan MP and Air Putih assemblyman alleged that a plot of land in Permatang Pauh had its land tax raised 2,722 times from RM25 to RM68,068, only to be reduced to RM12,155 after he intervened.
In a second case within his own Bagan parliamentary constituency, the land tax for a 258-unit low-cost flat in Bola-bola was raised five times from RM3,676 to RM22,120, before being cut to RM4,765 after Lim raised the matter publicly.
“If the land tax rate increase calculation system was indeed correct, fair, orderly and professional, what need would there be to reduce land tax by 80 per cent or 90 per cent?” he said.
Lim argued that reductions of such magnitude were conclusive proof that the state’s land tax calculation system was fundamentally flawed, failed and in urgent need of replacement, adding that such reductions proved the system was defective, failed and problematic.
He questioned why Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow continued to defend the professionalism and accuracy of Land and Mines Office officers when their own corrections told a very different story.
Lim said the pattern of repeated revisions exposed a systemic problem rather than isolated clerical mistakes, and called for the entire calculation framework to be reviewed and rebuilt from scratch.
He insisted that any new land tax calculation system must be developed only after thorough prior consultation with all relevant stakeholders, including landowners, residents and industry representatives.
Lim cited additional cases of extreme tax increases affecting ordinary residents, including a kampung house in Juru whose land tax rose 2,842 times from RM12 to RM34,118, and a 57-year-old man whose ancestral land tax surged 727 times from RM29 to RM21,103.
He also highlighted the case of a 62-year-old businessman from Simpang Empat, Seberang Perai Selatan, whose land tax on two plots quadrupled from RM6,901 to RM34,715, threatening to wipe out his livelihood amid falling business orders and mounting bank loan pressure.
“Why then is [the state] still defending government officers who made mistakes over the rakyat who have been wronged?” Lim demanded.
He further challenged Chow’s claim of being the state’s most hardworking elected representative, pointing out that the chief minister had reportedly received few direct complaints about the land tax hikes during his statewide tours.
Lim said it was meaningless to tour the entire state while remaining inaccessible to voters in Chow’s own Batu Kawan parliamentary constituency, where many of the worst-affected landowners reside.
He stressed that the scale and frequency of downward revisions made it impossible for the state government to credibly maintain that the original calculations were correct, fair or professionally executed.
“The chief minister should be reminded of the spirit of the struggle in conjunction with the DAP party’s 60th anniversary. The rakyat must be prioritised!” Lim concluded.

Title: Motorcyclist assaulted with baseball bat in Kulai after ‘terawih’ prayers
Article:
KULAI, March 20 — A 33-year-old man was assaulted by a group of men with a steel baseball bat near the Bukit Batu Community Centre here last Tuesday, shortly after performing his
terawih
prayers.
The incident, which occurred at 9.50pm, allegedly stemmed from a road altercation where the victim was accused of cutting into the assailants’ car.
Kulai district police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee confirmed that the victim had lodged a report and that police were aware of a video of the incident circulating online that matches the report.
“As a result of the assault, the victim suffered a head injury requiring five stitches, as well as other injuries to his body,” Tan said in a statement today.
“Investigators are actively tracking down the four suspects involved.”
According to initial investigations, the victim was riding his motorcycle home from a nearby surau when he crossed into the path of a grey Proton Saga.
“The car was then driven recklessly before it intercepted the victim, forcing him to stop,” Tan explained.
“Four unknown men alighted from the car and hit the victim on the head with a baseball bat, causing him to collapse.”
Tan added that the other assailants were then seen stomping on the victim before snatching his motorcycle keys and fleeing the scene.
The case is being investigated under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt with a dangerous weapon and Section 279 of the same code for reckless driving.
“Efforts to track down the suspects are ongoing,” he reiterated.
Police have urged anyone with information to assist the investigation by calling the Kulai district police hotline at 07-663 2222 or contacting the investigating officer, Inspector R. Harinnath, at 017-500 5731.
Tan also advised the public to refrain from speculating on the case, as it could hamper the investigation.

Title: Three die after Proton X70 collides with Alza on Pan Borneo Highway
Article:
KUCHING, March 20 — Three people were reportedly killed and five others injured in a crash involving two vehicles on the Pan Borneo Highway along the Serian–Sri Aman route near Kampung Selepong Jinggau today.
The incident involved a Proton X70 and a Perodua Alza, and was reported to the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) at about 8.30am, according to
Berita Harian
.
“Two boys in the same vehicle sustained head injuries and were given initial treatment by the Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team before being sent to Sri Aman Hospital,” a JBPM spokesperson said.
JBPM said the three victims who died were travelling in the Proton X70 — Lau Sie Tung, 70, and two women, Grace Lau Chi Ai, 22, and Lau Yok Leh, 22.
A team from the Sri Aman Fire and Rescue Station, located about 21km away, was dispatched to the scene and found the Perodua Alza engulfed in flames upon arrival.
Three occupants of the Alza — an adult woman and two boys — survived with minor injuries, although the vehicle was completely destroyed in the fire, and all injured victims were taken to Sri Aman Hospital for further treatment.
The operation ended at 10am after firefighting and clearing works were completed.

Title: PLUS expects heavy traffic until night as festive travel peaks ahead of Aidilfitri
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad reportedly expects traffic on the country’s major highways to remain heavy until late tonight as people travel back to their hometowns for Aidilfitri.
Its senior general manager of operations Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz said daily traffic still exceeded two million vehicles despite a slight drop from the previous day, according to a report in
Berita Harian.
“PLUS expects the same trend to continue today until late at night, as tomorrow is Aidilfitri,” he was quoted as saying.
He reportedly said that accidents have been handled quickly through cooperation between PLUS, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Road Transport Department (JPJ).
Traffic monitoring and dispersal are reportedly being carried out at identified hotspots, with Emergency Response Teams stationed at strategic locations along highways to respond swiftly to incidents.
Motorists were advised to plan their journeys in advance and check real-time updates via social media and the MyPLUS-TTA application to avoid congestion.

Title: Melaka food delivery rider remanded over violent attack on pregnant wife
Article:
MELAKA, March 20 — A man who beat his pregnant wife on Sunday has been remanded for four days at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here, starting today.
The remand order until Monday (March 23) against the food delivery person was issued by Magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani.
It is to assist the investigation under Section 323 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum imprisonment of one year or a fine of up to RM2,000 or both, and Section 18(A) of the Domestic Violence Act 1994.
Yesterday, the police arrested a man who was accused of beating his pregnant wife, in a house in Taman Seri Kemendor, Jasin.
Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit was reported to have said that the 22-year-old suspect was arrested by a team of police officers from the D8 and D11 units, Criminal Investigation Department (CID), Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) at around 11 am. — Bernama

Title: In Aidilfitri greetings, MCCBCHST calls for national unity and social cohesion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 – The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has extended its greetings to the Muslim community as the nation prepares to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on March 21.
In a statement released today, the interfaith council described the festival as a significant time for reflection, gratitude, and renewal.
MCCBCHST also said that racial and religious harmony remains the bedrock of Malaysia’s peace, stability, and ongoing progress.
“These foundations have enabled our diverse society to thrive and must be preserved with care and commitment by all segments of society,” the statement read.
In its message, the council urged Malaysians to continue fostering mutual respect and goodwill.
The group also made a specific appeal to leaders at all levels to champion unity and uphold the principles that maintain national integrity.
“At the same time, it is important that any actions or expressions that may undermine harmony and social cohesion be addressed prudently and in accordance with the law, in order to safeguard the stability and unity that Malaysians hold dear,” it said.
“As we celebrate this festive season, MCCBCHST expresses its hope that Hari Raya Aidilfitri will further strengthen the bonds of harmony among Malaysians.”

Title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert for several districts in Sabah, Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until noon today.
In a statement, MetMalaysia said that in Sabah, the areas expected to be affected are Tawau (Tawau and Semporna), while in Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu and Dalat). — Bernama

Title: Scammers use fake KL traffic police letterhead to solicit Raya donations, public warned
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) has denied issuing an invitation letter for a Hari Raya Aidilfitri event that also asked for donations from the public.
Kuala Lumpur JSPT chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa in a statement today said that his department detected the distribution of the fake letter via WhatsApp application at 5.50 pm yesterday.
According to him, the letter used an official letterhead purporting to be from the Traffic and Transport Branch of the Kuala Lumpur police contingent headquarters (IPK) and was signed by an individual using the name ‘Supt Jamil bin Abdul’.
“A check found that the name did not exist in the officer records at the Traffic and Transport Branch of the Kuala Lumpur police station or in the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM).
“ Kuala Lumpur JSPT stressed that no official letter was issued for the purpose of requesting donations from any party,” he said.
Mohd Zamzuri said the distribution of the letter was believed to be a fraudulent attempt aimed at deceiving the public to obtain donations as well as potentially damaging the image and credibility of the PDRM.
In this regard, a police report has been made to enable further investigation to be carried out under the relevant legal provisions.
“The public is advised to always be vigilant and not be fooled by any dubious donation requests.
“Any confirmation can be made directly with the Kuala Lumpur JSPT at 03-2071 9999, the Kuala Lumpur JSPT Hotline at 03-2026 0267/ 0269 or any nearby police station,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Raya exodus: LLM says most routes under control except two key highways
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Traffic flow on several major highways was slow this morning as people began returning to their hometowns to celebrate Aidilfitri tomorrow.
A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said that as of 8 am, congestion was reported at only two main locations – the East Coast Expressway (LPT) and the North-South Expressway (PLUS) – while traffic on other routes, including those heading south, remained under control.
“On the LPT, traffic is slow between Lintang and Bentong due to a surge in the number of vehicles. Congestion has also been detected on the KL-Karak Expressway heading east, with slow-moving traffic stretching 9.8 kilometres (km) from KM48.1 to KM57.9 (Bukit Tinggi).
